key_metrics: Medical costs increased 21.2% YoY to $79.96 billion
Earnings from operations fell 50.4% YoY to $4.32 billion
Diluted EPS dropped to $2.59 from $6.51 in the prior year quarter
qoq_changes: Total assets increased to $315.27 billion from $298.28 billion at year-end 2024
Medical costs payable increased to $40.18 billion from $34.22 billion at year-end 2024
new_risks: Significant margin compression due to medical costs growing much faster (21.2%) than premium revenue (14.9%)
Losses on sale of subsidiaries ($83 million in Q3 2025) indicating potential portfolio restructuring headwinds
management_tone: Cautious, as robust double-digit top-line growth was completely overshadowed by a severe spike in medical costs that crushed operating margins and net income.
analyst_note: Despite strong revenue growth of 12.2% YoY to $113.2B, UNH faces severe profitability headwinds as medical costs surged 21.2%, driving a 61% drop in net income. Investors will need to monitor utilization rates and pricing adjustments closely in upcoming quarters to gauge margin recovery.
